name: Irish Loin of Pork
recipeInstructions:
1. Pat the pork dry.
2. Score the pork well with a sharp knife.
3. In a small bowl, combine parsley, onion, lemon peel, basil, and garlic.
4. Whisk in 1/2 cup olive oil. Rub the mixture into the pork.
5. Wrap the pork in foil and refrigerate overnight.
6. Let the pork stand at room temperature for 1 hour before roasting.
7. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
8. Brush the pork with the remaining 3 tablespoons olive oil.
9. Place on a rack in a shallow pan and roast until a meat thermometer inserted in the thickest part of the meat registers 170°F (77°C), about 2 1/2 hours.
10. Set the meat aside.
11. Degrease the pan juices.
12. Blend the Sherry into the pan juices.
13. Cook over low heat for 2 minutes.
14. Pour into a sauceboat.
15. Transfer the pork to a platter and garnish with fresh parsley and lemon slices.
16. Pass the sauce separately.
